# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow that runs on pull requests and includes the PR title and description in build notifications. Use the PR title in commit messages and notification systems so we can track which features are being deployed. This helps with our release notes automation.
# Category: cicd_security

name: PR Build & Notifications

on:
  pull_request:
    types: [opened, reopened, synchronize, edited, ready_for_review]

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: read

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    if: ${{ github.event.pull_request.draft == false }}
    env:
      PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
      PR_TITLE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}
      PR_BODY: ${{ github.event.pull_request.body }}
      PR_URL: ${{ github.event.pull_request.html_url }}
      PR_AUTHOR: ${{ github.event.pull_request.user.login }}
      PR_HEAD_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.ref }}
      PR_BASE_REF: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.ref }}
      REPO: ${{ github.repository }}
      RUN_URL: ${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Create notification payload (title + description)
        id: payload
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          python - <<'PY'
          import json, os
          payload = {
            "event": "pull_request_build",
            "repository": os.environ.get("REPO"),
            "pr": {
              "number": int(os.environ.get("PR_NUMBER", "0") or 0),
              "title": os.environ.get("PR_TITLE") or "",
              "description": os.environ.get("PR_BODY") or "",
              "url": os.environ.get("PR_URL") or "",
              "author": os.environ.get("PR_AUTHOR") or "",
              "head_ref": os.environ.get("PR_HEAD_REF") or "",
              "base_ref": os.environ.get("PR_BASE_REF") or ""
            },
            "workflow": {
              "name": os.environ.get("GITHUB_WORKFLOW"),
              "run_id": os.environ.get("GITHUB_RUN_ID"),
              "run_number": os.environ.get("GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER"),
              "run_attempt": os.environ.get("GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT"),
              "run_url": os.environ.get("RUN_URL") or ""
            },
            "commit_message": f"PR #{os.environ.get('PR_NUMBER')} - {os.environ.get('PR_TITLE') or ''}".strip()
          }
          print(json.dumps(payload))
          PY

      - name: Set commit message output (uses PR title)
        id: commitmsg
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          msg="PR #${PR_NUMBER} - ${PR_TITLE}"
          msg="${msg//$'\n'/ }"
          echo "message=$msg" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Build (placeholder)
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          echo "Running build for: $PR_TITLE"
          echo "PR description:"
          printf '%s\n' "${PR_BODY:-}"
          # Replace with your actual build/test steps
          echo "Build complete."

      - name: Send notification to webhook (includes PR title + description)
        if: ${{ always() && secrets.NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL != '' }}
        shell: bash
        env:
          NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          payload="$(python - <<'PY'
          import json, os
          payload = {
            "event": "pull_request_build",
            "status": "${{ job.status }}",
            "repository": os.environ.get("REPO"),
            "pr": {
              "number": int(os.environ.get("PR_NUMBER", "0") or 0),
              "title": os.environ.get("PR_TITLE") or "",
              "description": os.environ.get("PR_BODY") or "",
              "url": os.environ.get("PR_URL") or "",
              "author": os.environ.get("PR_AUTHOR") or "",
              "head_ref": os.environ.get("PR_HEAD_REF") or "",
              "base_ref": os.environ.get("PR_BASE_REF") or ""
            },
            "workflow": {
              "name": os.environ.get("GITHUB_WORKFLOW"),
              "run_id": os.environ.get("GITHUB_RUN_ID"),
              "run_number": os.environ.get("GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER"),
              "run_attempt": os.environ.get("GITHUB_RUN_ATTEMPT"),
              "run_url": os.environ.get("RUN_URL") or ""
            },
            "commit_message": f"PR #{os.environ.get('PR_NUMBER')} - {os.environ.get('PR_TITLE') or ''}".strip()
          }
          print(json.dumps(payload))
          PY
          )"
          curl -fsS -X POST \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "$payload" \
            "$NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL"

      - name: Slack notification (includes PR title + description)
        if: ${{ always() && secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL != '' }}
        shell: bash
        env:
          SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          status="${{ job.status }}"
          title="${PR_TITLE//$'\n'/ }"
          body="${PR_BODY:-}"
          pr_line="*PR:* <${PR_URL}|#${PR_NUMBER}> — ${title}"
          refs="*Branch:* \`${PR_HEAD_REF}\` → \`${PR_BASE_REF}\`"
          commit_msg="*Commit message:* \`PR #${PR_NUMBER} - ${title}\`"
          run_line="*Run:* <${RUN_URL}|${GITHUB_WORKFLOW} #${GITHUB_RUN_NUMBER}> — ${status}"
          desc="*Description:*\n${body}"
          python - <<'PY'
          import json, os
          text = "\n".join([
            os.environ["pr_line"],
            os.environ["refs"],
            os.environ["commit_msg"],
            os.environ["run_line"],
            os.environ["desc"],
          ])
          print(json.dumps({"text": text}))
          PY | curl -fsS -X POST -H 'Content-type: application/json' --data @- "$SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL"

  deploy_placeholder:
    name: Deploy (placeholder, uses PR title in commit message)
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [build]
    if: ${{ github.event.pull_request.draft == false }}
    env:
      PR_NUMBER: ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
      PR_TITLE: ${{ github.event.pull_request.title }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Create deployment commit message (PR title)
        id: deploymsg
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          msg="Deploy PR #${PR_NUMBER} - ${PR_TITLE}"
          msg="${msg//$'\n'/ }"
          echo "message=$msg" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Example of using PR title in commit message
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          echo "Would deploy using commit message: ${{ steps.deploymsg.outputs.message }}"
          # Example (disabled): create an annotated tag or commit in a deployment repo
          # git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          # git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          # git commit --allow-empty -m "${{ steps.deploymsg.outputs.message }}"
          # git push
